message:
feat(demo): files page and test support enhancements

- files.rs: file browser page improvements
- mod.rs: page module updates
- placeholder.rs: placeholder refinements
- test_support.rs: test utilities expansion

Syncs beads tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -213,7 +213,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-8wrz","title":"huh: Complete Select field filter support","description":"# Task: Complete Select Field Filter Support

## Problem Statement
The Select field has incomplete filter support:

```rust
// Line 1610
pub struct Select<T> {
    #[allow(dead_code)]
    filtering: bool,       // Marked dead_code - not used!
    filter_value: String,  // Has filter value
    // ...
}
```

The field has filter_value and calls filtered_options() in view(), but:
1. `filtering` field is marked dead_code (never used)
2. Navigation (up/down) doesn't account for filtered state
3. No UI for entering filter text
4. Inconsistent with MultiSelect which has full filter support

## Current State Analysis
- filtered_options() exists and works
- view() renders filtered list
- Navigation uses unfiltered indices
- No keyboard handler for filter input
- filtering flag unused

## Expected Behavior (based on MultiSelect)
1. Type to filter - typing characters filters visible options
2. Navigation within filtered set
3. Selection maintains correct item reference
4. Clear filter resets to full list

## Solution Design

### Option A: Full Filter Support (Match MultiSelect)
1. Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from filtering
2. Add filter state transition on typing
3. Update navigation to use filtered indices
4. Add filter UI element

### Option B: Remove Unused Filter Infrastructure
If filtering isn't intended for Select:
1. Remove filter_value, filtering fields
2. Remove filtered_options() call
3. Simplify to always show all options
4. Document that Select doesn't support filtering

### Recommended: Option A
Select fields with many options benefit from filtering.
MultiSelect already has the pattern implemented.

## Implementation Steps (Option A)
1. Enable filtering field, set via builder
2. Add key handler for typing to filter
3. Update navigation to work with filtered indices
4. Add filter display in view()
5. Port relevant logic from MultiSelect
6. Add tests for filtering behavior

## Code Changes Required
```rust
impl Select<T> {
    // Builder method
    pub fn filterable(mut self, filterable: bool) -> Self {
        self.filtering = filterable;
        self
    }
    
    // In update(), handle typing when filterable
    fn handle_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> Option<Cmd> {
        if self.filtering {
            match key.code {
                KeyCode::Char(c) => {
                    self.filter_value.push(c);
                    self.update_filtered();
                    return None;
                }
                KeyCode::Backspace => {
                    self.filter_value.pop();
                    self.update_filtered();
                    return None;
                }
                // ...
            }
        }
        // Normal navigation...
    }
    
    fn update_filtered(&mut self) {
        let filtered = self.filtered_options();
        // Ensure cursor is within filtered bounds
        if self.cursor >= filtered.len() {
            self.cursor = filtered.len().saturating_sub(1);
        }
    }
}
```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn select_filter_basic() {
    let mut select = Select::new(vec!["Apple", "Apricot", "Banana"])
        .filterable(true);
    
    // Type "ap" to filter
    select.update(KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('a')));
    select.update(KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('p')));
    
    // Should show Apple, Apricot (not Banana)
    let view = select.view();
    assert!(view.contains("Apple"));
    assert!(view.contains("Apricot"));
    assert!(!view.contains("Banana"));
}

#[test]
fn select_filter_navigation() {
    let mut select = Select::new(vec!["Cat", "Car", "Dog"])
        .filterable(true);
    
    // Filter to "ca" (Cat, Car)
    select.filter_value = "ca".to_string();
    select.update_filtered();
    
    // Navigate down
    select.update(KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Down));
    
    // Should be on Car (filtered index 1)
    assert_eq!(select.selected(), Some(&"Car"));
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Select supports optional filtering (builder method)
- [ ] Typing filters options when enabled
- [ ] Navigation works correctly within filtered set
- [ ] Selection returns correct item
- [ ] Filter can be cleared
- [ ] Tests cover filter + navigation + selection
- [ ] Consistent with MultiSelect behavior","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:22:08.978015419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:23:22.658212749Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filtering","huh","select"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8wrz","depends_on_id":"bd-17p2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:23:22.658170230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-8wrz","depends_on_id":"bd-2xzk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:22:08.996937216Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-97j0","title":"Integration Tests: Terminal I/O verification","description":"Integration tests that verify actual terminal ANSI output.

## Purpose
Ensure rendered output produces correct ANSI escape sequences
that work in real terminals.

## Test Infrastructure

### PTY Test Harness
- [ ] Create pseudo-terminal for test
- [ ] Capture raw ANSI output
- [ ] Parse and verify sequences
- [ ] Support both sync and async tests

### ANSI Verification
- [ ] Color sequences (16, 256, RGB)
- [ ] Style sequences (bold, italic, etc.)
- [ ] Cursor movement sequences
- [ ] Screen clear sequences
- [ ] Alternate screen buffer

## Test Requirements

### lipgloss Output
- [ ] Style::render() produces valid ANSI
- [ ] Nested styles compose correctly
- [ ] Reset sequences properly placed

### bubbletea Screen
- [ ] Initial render clears and positions
- [ ] Update render only changes dirty regions
- [ ] Final cleanup restores terminal

### glamour Output
- [ ] Markdown styles render correctly
- [ ] Code blocks have syntax colors
- [ ] Links styled appropriately

## Success Criteria
- All output parseable by ANSI parser
- No raw/unescaped control characters
- Terminal state properly restored","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:37.504476203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:37.523952606Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","terminal","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-97j0","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:37.523928221Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ab35","title":"demo_showcase core: App model + routing + message taxonomy","description":"Design + implement the top-level `App` model.

Define:
- AppState: current page, focus target, global toggles (animations/mouse), theme, notifications.
- Message taxonomy: routeable app messages vs component messages; minimize ad-hoc `Any` downcasts.
- Routing: an enum of pages + a clean delegation pattern (App calls page.update/view).

Acceptance:
- Adding a new page is "mechanical": implement a page model and register it in router.
- Message routing is understandable and testable.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:56.311481194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:24:27.649972129Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:24:27.649906416Z","close_reason":"Implemented App model, routing, message taxonomy, theme system, and page framework","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ab35","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:11.958931843Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ab35","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:56.340015687Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ab35","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:10.685669509Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-adw0","title":"Property Tests: harmonica spring physics","description":"Property-based tests for harmonica spring animation physics.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify spring physics invariants
and find edge cases through random input generation.

## Properties to Test

### Convergence
- [ ] For any (stiffness > 0, damping > 0), animation converges
- [ ] Convergence time bounded for given parameters
- [ ] Final value equals target within tolerance

### Stability
- [ ] No NaN or Inf values for any input
- [ ] Values don't explode (bounded growth)
- [ ] Consistent results for same input

### Physical Correctness
- [ ] More stiffness = faster response
- [ ] More damping = less oscillation
- [ ] Zero damping = sustained oscillation
- [ ] Critical damping = no overshoot

### Frame Independence
- [ ] Result similar regardless of frame rate
- [ ] Large dt doesn't break physics
- [ ] Small dt accumulated = large dt single

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn spring_always_converges(
        stiffness in 0.1..1000.0f64,
        damping in 0.1..100.0f64,
        target in -1000.0..1000.0f64,
    ) {
        let spring = Spring::new(stiffness, damping);
        // Simulate many frames
        // Assert converges to target
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- No crashes for any random input
- Physics invariants always hold
- Edge cases documented and handled","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:52.362702645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:56.297788477Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["harmonica","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-adw0","depends_on_id":"bd-228s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:56.297757189Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-adw0","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:52.397263463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
